details: Houthis launched 100+ attacks affecting shipping linked to 60+ countries.
July 2025: Magic Seas and Eternity C were sunk, marking major escalation.
Gaza ceasefire contributed to a pause in Houthi attacks.
A relative calm period lasted about 3.5 months.
US-Israel strikes on Iran (Operation Epic Fury + Roaring Lion).
Iran moved to block Hormuz Strait; traffic fell 81% within 24 hours.
Houthi attacks resumed in the Red Sea.
Hormuz, Bab el-Mandeb, and Suez simultaneously degraded or disrupted.

mapEvents.name: Hormuz traffic collapse
mapEvents.description: Iranian closure actions drove an 81% traffic drop in 24 hours; 150+ ships reported delayed or stranded.

mapEvents.name: Bab el-Mandeb attacks resume
mapEvents.description: Houthi attacks resumed against Red Sea shipping after the prior pause.

mapEvents.name: Suez corridor collapse
mapEvents.description: Commercial routing through Suez remained functionally impaired with most operators rerouting via the Cape.

mapEvents.name: Tanker attack cluster
mapEvents.description: Three tankers were reported attacked during the Hormuz crisis window.

mapEvents.name: Djibouti approach threat
mapEvents.description: Approaches to the southern Red Sea remained exposed to missile and drone threats.
